state.projectTitle: Agile vs Waterfall Arena: Project Survival Simulation
state.deliverableArea: SDLC Phase Manager, Agile/Scrum Sprint Simulator
state.problemStatement: Many undergraduate IT Project Management students struggle to understand when to apply Agile or Waterfall methodologies in real-world scenarios. Studies and course observations indicate that approximately 60% of students can recall theoretical differences but fail to make correct methodology decisions in practical cases. This gap leads to poor project planning, inefficient resource allocation, and increased project risks. The proposed gamification system addresses this issue by providing an interactive simulation where students actively experience decision-making under varying project conditions. Through this approach, students are expected to develop higher-order cognitive skills aligned with Bloom’s taxonomy (Analyze, Evaluate, Create).
state.targetPopulation: 3rd or 4th year undergraduate students enrolled in IT Project Management
successCriteria: At least 70% of participants correctly select the appropriate project management methodology based on scenario conditions.
Average methodology selection accuracy increases by at least 25% between pre-test and post-test assessments.
At least 80% of participants successfully complete all project simulation scenarios within the allocated session time.
Decision-making performance related to project constraints (time, budget, quality, and risk) improves by at least 20% across multiple rounds.
At least 75% of participants correctly identify requirement volatility and stakeholder complexity during scenario analysis.
Average post-session project management assessment scores improve by at least 30% compared to baseline results.
At least 70% of participants provide evidence-based justifications for their methodology choices during reflection activities.
More than 80% of participants actively participate in collaborative discussion and peer-feedback activities during gameplay sessions.
At least 85% of participants report improved confidence in applying Agile and Waterfall methodologies to real-world project cases.
Course performance related to project management case-study questions improves by at least one letter-grade equivalent after implementation of the gamification system.

bloomOutcomes.outcome: Students will APPLY appropriate software development methodology (Agile vs Waterfall) based on given project scenarios
bloomOutcomes.assessment: Decision accuracy rate per scenario (% correct selections)

bloomOutcomes.outcome: Students will ANALYSE project characteristics (requirement stability, risk, stakeholder involvement)
Students will EVALUATE different methodologies and justify their decisions
Students will CREATE optimal project strategies under varying conditions
Students will REMEMBER the fundamental characteristics of Agile and Waterfall methodologies.
bloomOutcomes.assessment: Quiz accuracy score on terminology and methodology features

bloomOutcomes.outcome: Students will UNDERSTAND how project conditions influence methodology selection.
bloomOutcomes.assessment: Short written explanation evaluated with rubric scoring

state.narrativeTheme: A global software company is struggling to manage multiple complex digital transformation projects under strict deadlines, changing customer demands, limited budgets, and increasing stakeholder pressure. Players take the role of project managers responsible for choosing the most suitable project management methodology for each business case. Some projects require rapid iteration and flexible planning, while others demand strict documentation, stable requirements, and controlled execution. Throughout the simulation, players analyse risks, allocate resources, respond to unexpected project events, and balance quality, time, and budget constraints. The game integrates Self-Determination Theory through meaningful strategic choices, Mechanics-Dynamics-Aesthetics principles through interactive gameplay systems, and Bloom Taxonomy through higher-order analysis and evaluation tasks. HEXAD Player Types are supported through collaborative, competitive, and exploratory mechanics, while the Kirkpatrick Evaluation Framework measures engagement, learning transfer, and educational effectiveness across gameplay sessions.
state.gameOverview: The game simulates real-world project management decision-making through interactive scenario-based challenges. Players analyse project characteristics such as requirement stability, stakeholder involvement, risk level, budget pressure, and delivery urgency before selecting Agile or Waterfall methodologies. The system combines strategic decision-making, adaptive gameplay, scoring systems, feedback loops, and collaborative discussion to reinforce learning outcomes. The design integrates Self-Determination Theory, Mechanics-Dynamics-Aesthetics, HEXAD player motivations, Bloom Taxonomy cognitive levels, and the Kirkpatrick four-level evaluation approach to create both engaging gameplay and measurable educational impact.
state.sdtAutonomy: Players are given meaningful choices by selecting Agile or Waterfall methodologies based on project conditions. They can also decide which methodology-specific actions to apply during each scenario, such as Sprint, Retrospective, Gantt Chart, or Gate Review. This gives players control over their strategic path and allows them to experience the consequences of their own decisions.
state.sdtCompetence: Competence is supported through progressive difficulty and feedback-driven learning. Early scenarios present simpler and more predictable project conditions, while later scenarios introduce volatile requirements, higher risks, and stakeholder complexity. Players improve by learning which methodology fits which context, and they receive immediate feedback that reinforces mastery and better decision-making.
state.sdtRelatedness: Relatedness is supported through team-based discussion, comparison of strategies, and shared reflection on project outcomes. Players can collaborate in small groups or compare their methodology choices with others, which encourages communication and peer learning. This social interaction helps students understand that project management decisions are often made collectively in real-world environments.

rewardStructure.elite: Executive project leader badge, priority scenario access, strategic voting rights for special missions, and exclusive high-level achievement rewards.
rewardStructure.professional: Certificate bonuses, methodology mastery badges, advanced training materials, mentorship opportunities, and additional project simulation scenarios.
rewardStructure.access: Participation credits, cosmetic profile customisation items, basic achievement badges, leaderboard visibility, and discussion forum privileges.

conceptMappings.dynamic: Strategic decision-making based on scenario conditions
conceptMappings.rationale: Players learn when to use each methodology by applying it in context
Demonstrates how Agile performs better in dynamic environments

mdaAnalysis.mechanics: The main mechanics include scenario-based decision-making, methodology selection (Agile or Waterfall), methodology-specific action cards, project metrics (time, budget, quality, and risk), scoring rules, and feedback after each round. Players interact with structured project situations and use strategic choices to influence the final project outcome.
mdaAnalysis.dynamics: These mechanics create dynamics such as strategic comparison, trade-off analysis, adaptive decision-making, and risk-response behaviour. As project conditions change, players learn that one methodology does not fit every scenario. This encourages experimentation, reflection, and deeper understanding of project constraints and methodology suitability.
mdaAnalysis.aesthetics: The desired player experience includes challenge, strategic thinking, tension, mastery, and satisfaction. Players should feel engaged as they try to make the best possible methodology choice under changing project conditions. The game aims to create a sense of accomplishment when players successfully align project strategy with scenario needs.

scenarios.name: Main Scenario
scenarios.title: Startup Project: Changing Requirements
scenarios.setup:
A startup company is developing a mobile application.
The client frequently changes requirements and expects rapid iterations.
Resources are limited, and time-to-market is critical.
scenarios.objective: Players must select the most appropriate development methodology and manage the project effectively under changing requirements.
scenarios.winCondition: Correctly identify project characteristics AND select Agile methodology AND maintain project success indicators above threshold.
scenarios.loseCondition: Selecting an inappropriate methodology (e.g., Waterfall) OR failing to manage project constraints (time, quality).
scenarios.storyboard:
Players take the role of a project manager in a startup environment.
They analyze project conditions (uncertainty, stakeholder behavior, time pressure) and decide which methodology to apply.
The system simulates project outcomes based on their decisions.
scenarios.algorithm: Rule-based decision engine:  If requirements = dynamic → Agile preferred If feedback loops used → success probability increases If rigid planning chosen → failure risk increases
scenarios.outcome: Requirement changes occur randomly Client feedback timing varies Resource constraints slightly fluctuate

activities.description: Player evaluates requirements stability, stakeholder behavior, and project constraints
Player balances time, budget, and quality

scenarios.name: Alternative Scenario
scenarios.title: Government Project: Fixed Requirements
scenarios.setup:
A government project with clearly defined requirements, strict documentation, and minimal expected changes.
Deadlines and compliance are critical.
scenarios.objective: Select the most suitable methodology for a stable and predictable project.
scenarios.winCondition: Correctly identify stable requirements AND select Waterfall AND complete project within constraints.
scenarios.loseCondition: Selecting Agile unnecessarily OR mismanaging structured workflow.
scenarios.storyboard:
Players act as a project manager in a highly regulated environment.
They must follow strict processes and ensure full documentation and predictability.
scenarios.algorithm: Stable requirements → Waterfall preferred High documentation → increases success Agile misuse → inefficiency
scenarios.outcome: Minor delays in approval processes Small budget constraints Documentation requirements variation

scenarios.title: Enterprise System Migration: High Risk Transformation
scenarios.setup: A large enterprise plans to migrate its legacy infrastructure to a cloud-based system. The project involves multiple departments, strict security requirements, budget limitations, and high operational risks. Stakeholders expect minimal downtime during migration.
scenarios.winCondition: Correctly analyse project risks and stakeholder complexity AND select the most suitable hybrid or phased methodology AND maintain acceptable quality and risk indicators throughout the migration process.
scenarios.loseCondition: Selecting an unsuitable methodology OR ignoring critical migration risks. No punitive scoring is applied; players receive corrective feedback and can retry alternative strategies.
scenarios.outcome:
Unexpected security issues may emerge.
Stakeholder priorities may change during migration.
Budget and downtime constraints dynamically affect decisions.
scenarios.neutralCondition: Project partially succeeds but requires additional iterations and stakeholder negotiations before completion.

userStories.want: select the appropriate project management methodology based on project conditions
userStories.soThat: I can understand when to use Agile or Waterfall in real-world scenarios
userStories.acc: Given a project scenario with defined characteristics When I choose a methodology Then the system should evaluate my decision based on scenario suitability

userStories.want: apply methodology-specific actions (e.g., Sprint, Gantt Chart)
userStories.soThat: I can see how each methodology affects project outcomes
userStories.acc: Given available action options When I select an action Then project metrics (time, budget, quality, risk) should update accordingly

userStories.soThat: I can learn from my mistakes and improve my decision-making skills
userStories.acc: Given a completed scenario When results are displayed Then the system should explain why the chosen methodology was correct or incorrect

userStories.soThat: I can improve my project management skills over time  Acceptance

frs.desc: The system shall present different project scenarios with varying characteristics such as requirement stability, risk level, and stakeholder complexity.
The system shall allow players to choose between Agile and Waterfall methodologies at the beginning of each scenario.
The system shall provide methodology-specific action cards (e.g., Sprint, Retrospective, Gantt Chart, Gate Review).
The system shall update project metrics (time, budget, quality, risk) based on player decisions.
The system shall calculate a final project success score based on defined performance criteria.
The system shall provide immediate feedback explaining the impact of each decision on project outcomes.

nfrs.desc: The system shall be easy to learn and playable within 5 minutes of onboarding
nfrs.target: ≥80% of users complete first session without assistance

nfrs.desc: The system shall respond to user actions within 2 seconds.
The system shall be usable on standard web browsers without installation.
The system shall maintain user engagement for at least 20 minutes per session.
The system shall protect participant data using privacy-focused design principles and comply with basic GDPR/KVKK data protection requirements.
nfrs.target: 100% of gameplay records are anonymised or pseudonymised, and no personally identifiable information is stored in gameplay logs.

state.gameDuration: Total session duration is approximately 60 minutes: 10 minutes introduction and rule explanation, 35 minutes scenario gameplay across multiple project cases, 10 minutes reflection and discussion, and 5 minutes final feedback and assessment.

sessionSteps.name: Introduction & Briefing
sessionSteps.description: Players receive an overview of Agile and Waterfall methodologies, game objectives, scoring rules, and project constraints before gameplay begins.

sessionSteps.name: Scenario Analysis
sessionSteps.description: Players analyse project characteristics such as requirement stability, stakeholder involvement, project risk, and budget constraints before selecting methodologies.

sessionSteps.name: Methodology Selection
sessionSteps.description: Players choose Agile or Waterfall methodologies and apply methodology-specific actions to maximise project success indicators.

sessionSteps.name: Dynamic Event Phase
sessionSteps.description: Unexpected project events such as requirement changes, stakeholder conflicts, delays, and budget risks dynamically alter gameplay conditions.

sessionSteps.name: Reflection & Discussion
sessionSteps.description: Players discuss their decisions, compare strategies, and evaluate the effectiveness of methodology choices through collaborative reflection.

sessionSteps.name: Feedback & Assessment
sessionSteps.description: The system provides performance analytics, scenario outcomes, learning feedback, and post-session evaluation results.

kpis.method: Post-session Likert-scale survey and usability feedback analysis

kpis.target: At least 25% improvement in assessment scores

kpis.metric: Behavioural transfer of project management decision-making skills
kpis.method: Observation of later assignments and scenario-based evaluations
kpis.target: At least 70% correct methodology application in later coursework

kpis.metric: Overall academic performance and project success improvement
kpis.method: Comparison of course outcomes with previous non-gamified cohorts
kpis.target: Average performance improvement equivalent to at least one letter grade

state.logPrivacy:
All gameplay data is anonymised using pseudonymisation techniques. 
Player identities are not directly stored in the system.

No sensitive or personal data (e.g., name, email, location) is collected.

Player IDs cannot be linked to real identities without a separate key file, which is securely stored and accessible only to authorised academic staff.

Data is used strictly for educational assessment and system improvement purposes.

Logs are retained for a maximum of 2 years for academic audit requirements, after which they are permanently deleted.

Players have the right to request data access or deletion at any time.

All data handling complies with basic data protection and privacy principles.

tuslerRisks.risk: Players losing motivation due to repeated mistakes
tuslerRisks.preventive: Use adaptive difficulty and hint system
tuslerRisks.responsive: Encourage retry with guidance and no harsh penalties

tuslerRisks.risk: Players losing motivation due to repeated mistakes
tuslerRisks.preventive: Use adaptive difficulty and hint system
tuslerRisks.responsive: Encourage retry with guidance and no harsh penalties
Restart scenario or provide backup access

tuslerRisks.risk: Minor confusion about rules or interface

tuslerRisks.mitigation: Mandatory pre-game compliance training to mitigate role-confusion risk

supportTeam.name: Nameee

state.facilitatorRole:
The facilitator acts as a guide, not a player.

Responsibilities:
- Explain game objectives and rules clearly at the beginning
- Monitor time and manage scenario transitions
- Answer rule-related questions (not strategy)
- Ensure system runs properly
- Lead reflection discussions after each scenario

The facilitator does NOT:
- Reveal correct answers during gameplay
- Influence player decisions
- Interfere with scoring system

state.inScope: Compassion model approach with no punitive scoring. Failure is treated as a guided learning opportunity supported by reflection, feedback, retry mechanics, and facilitator discussion. The project scope includes scenario-based methodology selection, performance tracking, collaborative analysis, adaptive feedback systems, and educational assessment features focused on Agile and Waterfall decision-making skills.
state.outScope: Real-time integration with external project management tools Advanced AI-driven adaptive gameplay Multiplayer online real-time system Full-scale enterprise project tracking features
state.whyMotivation: The primary motivation of this gamification proposal is to help students bridge the gap between theoretical knowledge and practical project management decision-making. Many students can memorise Agile and Waterfall concepts but struggle to apply them correctly in realistic situations. The game encourages active learning through scenario-based interaction, reflection, experimentation, and collaborative discussion. By combining challenge, feedback, autonomy, and progression systems, the design aims to increase engagement, critical thinking, and long-term retention of project management principles.
state.howEthics: The system follows a compassion model approach that prioritises learning over punishment. No punitive scoring is applied, and failure is treated as a learning opportunity supported by feedback and reflection. All gameplay logs are anonymised using pseudonymisation techniques and handled according to privacy and data protection principles. Participation data is used only for educational evaluation and system improvement purposes. The game design also supports inclusivity, accessibility, fairness, and respectful communication between participants.

sixDim.what: Points and levels are used to provide continuous feedback and reinforce learning progress aligned with SDT Competence. Quests and challenges represent different project scenarios where players must select appropriate methodologies and actions. Immediate feedback ensures that players understand the impact of their decisions in real time. Leaderboards are used in a non-punitive way to encourage performance comparison without discouraging lower-performing students. Roles and characters simulate real-world project roles, enhancing immersion and contextual learning.
sixDim.why: Player motivation is primarily driven by intrinsic factors aligned with Self-Determination Theory (SDT). Autonomy is supported by allowing players to choose methodologies and strategies. Competence is reinforced through progressive challenges and feedback that improves decision-making skills. Relatedness is encouraged through team-based comparison and shared reflection. Additionally, elements from HEXAD are integrated to address different player types, ensuring balanced engagement across achievers, socialisers, and explorers.
sixDim.who: Participation in the gamified system is voluntary and does not directly affect academic grades. All collected data is anonymised and used only for educational improvement and analysis purposes. The system avoids manipulative reward structures and does not penalise failure; instead, it provides constructive feedback to support learning. Leaderboards are designed to emphasise personal improvement rather than competition to prevent discouragement. The design ensures inclusivity by accommodating different player types and learning styles.
sixDim.when: The gamification is implemented during weekly class or lab sessions, typically lasting 60–90 minutes. Each session represents a complete gameplay cycle where players engage with one or more project scenarios. The pacing is designed to gradually increase complexity, starting with simple and predictable scenarios and progressing to more complex and dynamic situations. This ensures sustained engagement and progressive learning.
sixDim.how: To prevent pointsification, points are only awarded based on correct methodology selection and meaningful decision-making, not passive participation. The system avoids punishing failure by providing constructive feedback instead of negative scoring. Leaderboards are designed to be non-exclusionary by focusing on improvement rather than ranking alone. Engagement addiction is avoided by limiting session duration and avoiding exploitative reward patterns. Ethical considerations include voluntary participation, anonymised data collection, and ensuring that gameplay does not negatively impact academic grading.
sixDim.where: Browser-based web application developed using Python (Streamlit) or a simple web interface, accessible during class sessions. Optional use of projected dashboard for facilitator guidance.

_extensionFields.characters: Players take the role of Project Managers competing to successfully deliver projects. They must analyse project conditions, choose the appropriate methodology, and respond to dynamic challenges such as changing requirements, risks, and stakeholder demands.
_extensionFields.storyWorld: The game world is a simulated corporate environment where multiple projects are running simultaneously. Each project presents unique constraints such as requirement volatility, risk level, and stakeholder complexity. Players must navigate these conditions strategically, making decisions that directly impact project success.
_extensionFields.introText:
Welcome to Agile vs Waterfall Arena! Your objective is to successfully manage project scenarios by selecting the most appropriate project management methodology (Agile or Waterfall) based on project conditions.

This game simulates real-world project environments with varying levels of requirement stability, risk, and stakeholder complexity. The game consists of 4 rounds, each representing a different project scenario.

In each round, you will analyse the scenario, choose a methodology, and apply strategic decisions. You will have limited time to complete each round.

Your performance will be evaluated based on:
- Correct identification of project characteristics
- Selection of appropriate methodology
- Quality of decision justification
- Overall project success (time, budget, quality, risk)

You succeed when you complete projects within constraints and demonstrate strong decision-making skills. There are no harsh penalties — the goal is learning and improvement.
_extensionFields.preGameSetup:
Environment Configuration:
A browser-based simulation platform is pre-loaded with multiple project scenarios and decision options.

Materials:
Players access the system via a web link. Optional printed summaries of scenarios can be provided.

Initial Log:
The system logs player entry and tracks decisions, scores, and progress.

Facilitator Setup:
Ensure platform accessibility, scenario readiness, and projection for shared discussion.

Game Setup:
Each player starts with equal project resources (time, budget, quality baseline, risk level).

scoringSystem.basis:
+30 points for correctly identifying scenario characteristics
+40 points for selecting the appropriate methodology
+20 points for maintaining project quality above threshold
+10 points for completing within time and budget
scoringSystem.bonuses:
+10 points for optimal strategy (best possible decision for given scenario)
+10 points for correct decision justification during reflection phase
+5 points for consistent performance across multiple rounds
scoringSystem.penalties: No harsh penalties are applied. Incorrect decisions do not result in heavy score reduction. Instead, they trigger guided feedback explaining the correct methodology and reasoning. Minor score reduction may apply (-5 points) to indicate suboptimal decisions, but the focus remains on learning and improvement.
scoringSystem.badges:
Analyst Badge — correctly identifies scenario characteristics
Methodology Master — selects correct methodology in 3 consecutive scenarios
Adaptive Thinker — successfully handles dynamic (changing) scenarios
Strategic Planner — maintains balance across time, budget, and quality
Project Champion — achieves highest overall performance score

rewardTiers.elite:
Methodology Master Badge — awarded for 3 consecutive correct methodology decisions

Adaptive Strategist — successfully handles dynamic scenarios with changing requirements

Project Champion — achieves highest performance score across all scenarios

Unlock Advanced Scenarios — access to more complex and time-constrained projects
rewardTiers.professional:
XP (Experience Points) System — players earn points from each activity

Level Progression — Beginner → Intermediate → Advanced

Skill Feedback Reports — personalised insights on strengths and weaknesses

Scenario Unlock System — new scenarios unlocked based on performance
rewardTiers.access:
Participation Points — awarded for completing scenarios

Reflection Bonus — extra points for explaining decisions

Retry Tokens — allows players to replay scenarios without penalty

Hint Tokens — unlock guidance for difficult scenarios

kirkpatrick.l1:
Students report engagement and satisfaction  
KPI: ≥75% positive feedback in post-session survey
kirkpatrick.l2:
Students improve methodology selection accuracy  
KPI: ≥25% increase between pre-test and post-test
kirkpatrick.l3:
Students correctly apply methodology in later coursework  
KPI: ≥70% correct methodology selection in assignments 4 weeks later
kirkpatrick.l4:
Overall academic performance improves  
KPI: Average course grade improvement ≥ 0.5 letter grade

futureEnhancements: Introduce adaptive scenario complexity based on player performance
Add AI-assisted feedback for deeper explanation of decisions
Expand scenario library with real-world case studies
Integrate with LMS for automated grading and tracking
_extensionFields.dataCollection:
Quantitative Analysis:
Likert-scale and test scores analysed using descriptive statistics (mean, variance, improvement rate).
Qualitative Analysis:
Thematic coding applied to interviews, focus groups, and open-ended responses.
Triangulation:
Multiple data sources combined (analytics + surveys + interviews) to ensure validity and reliability.
Feedback Loop:
Monthly insights shared with stakeholders → iterative improvements applied.
_extensionFields.continuousImprovement: The system will be continuously improved based on collected data, player performance, and feedback to ensure long-term learning effectiveness.
_extensionFields.analyticsplatform: Game execution logs + LMS (e.g., Moodle) integration
_extensionFields.deliverableJust: The selected deliverable areas focus on simulating real-world project management environments where students must choose and apply appropriate methodologies. Agile/Scrum Sprint Simulator enables iterative decision-making under dynamic conditions, while SDLC Phase Manager supports structured planning scenarios aligned with Waterfall methodology. These areas directly address the learning objective of selecting suitable methodologies based on project characteristics and constraints.
_extensionFields.platform: Browser-based web application built using Python (Streamlit v1.x) or a lightweight web framework. The system will run locally or be hosted on a cloud platform (e.g., Render / Heroku alternative) for easy access. No installation is required; players access the simulation via a web browser during class sessions. A facilitator dashboard may be displayed via projector to guide gameplay and discussion.
_extensionFields.narrativeEvolution:
Week 1–2: Introduction and familiarisation with Agile and Waterfall concepts.
Week 3–6: Increasing complexity with dynamic project scenarios and decision-making challenges.
Week 7–10: Competitive phase where players optimise strategies across multiple scenarios.
Week 11–12: Final evaluation and reflection, analysing decisions and outcomes.
_extensionFields.socialRationale:
Social interaction is intentionally designed to support multiple motivational frameworks.

From a Self-Determination Theory (SDT) perspective, social features enhance relatedness by enabling collaboration, discussion, and peer learning. Study clubs and mentoring systems allow students to share strategies and support each other, reflecting real-world project environments.

From a HEXAD perspective, Socialisers are engaged through forums and group discussions, Achievers through leaderboard comparison, and Philanthropists through mentoring and helping others.

From an Octalysis perspective, Core Drive 5 (Social Influence & Relatedness) is activated through peer comparison, feedback, and shared experiences. Core Drive 2 (Accomplishment) is reinforced through leaderboard positioning and visible performance metrics.

These features are not included for engagement alone, but to create structured social learning that improves reflection, decision-making, and knowledge transfer.
_extensionFields.communityStandards:
All interactions must remain respectful, constructive, and academically focused.

Criticism must target decisions and strategies, not individuals. Personal attacks, harassment, or exclusionary behaviour are strictly prohibited.

All social features are moderated automatically (keyword filtering) and by instructors to ensure a safe learning environment.

Students are encouraged to provide evidence-based feedback, justify their decisions, and engage in reflective discussion.

Repeated violations result in temporary restriction from social features.

This policy ensures that social interaction enhances learning rather than creating pressure or negative competition.
_extensionFields.pricingModel: Social and reward systems are explicitly designed to support motivational theories (SDT, HEXAD, Octalysis), ensuring that engagement is driven by meaningful interaction, not superficial gamification elements.
_extensionFields.adaptiveDifficulty:
The system adapts difficulty based on player performance metrics:

- If a player repeatedly selects incorrect methodologies → 
  the system provides hints and simplified scenarios (e.g., clearer project conditions).

- If a player performs consistently well →
  more complex scenarios are introduced (mixed conditions, time pressure, ambiguous requirements).

- Time limits are dynamically adjusted:
  more time for struggling players, less time for advanced players.

This adaptation is rule-based and transparent to ensure fairness and feasibility.
_extensionFields.personalisation:
Personalised Feedback System:
After each scenario, players receive feedback explaining why their decision was correct or incorrect.

Weakness Detection:
The system identifies patterns (e.g., always choosing Agile incorrectly) and highlights learning gaps.

Scenario Recommendation:
Players are guided towards scenarios that target their weak areas.

Optional Customisation:
- Avatar selection (cosmetic only)
- UI preferences (dark/light mode)
- Notification settings

No manipulation or behavioural exploitation is used.

The system logs player entry and tracks decisions, scores, and progress.

Facilitator Setup:
Ensure platform accessibility, scenario readiness, and projection for shared discussion.

Game Setup:
Each player starts with equal project resources (time, budget, quality baseline, risk level).

scoringSystem.basis:
+30 points for correctly identifying scenario characteristics
+40 points for selecting the appropriate methodology
+20 points for maintaining project quality above threshold
+10 points for completing within time and budget
scoringSystem.bonuses:
+10 points for optimal strategy (best possible decision for given scenario)
+10 points for correct decision justification during reflection phase
+5 points for consistent performance across multiple rounds
scoringSystem.penalties: No harsh penalties are applied. Incorrect decisions do not result in heavy score reduction. Instead, they trigger guided feedback explaining the correct methodology and reasoning. Minor score reduction may apply (-5 points) to indicate suboptimal decisions, but the focus remains on learning and improvement.
scoringSystem.badges:
Analyst Badge — correctly identifies scenario characteristics
Methodology Master — selects correct methodology in 3 consecutive scenarios
Adaptive Thinker — successfully handles dynamic (changing) scenarios
Strategic Planner — maintains balance across time, budget, and quality
Project Champion — achieves highest overall performance score

rewardTiers.elite:
Methodology Master Badge — awarded for 3 consecutive correct methodology decisions

Adaptive Strategist — successfully handles dynamic scenarios with changing requirements

Project Champion — achieves highest performance score across all scenarios

Unlock Advanced Scenarios — access to more complex and time-constrained projects
rewardTiers.professional:
XP (Experience Points) System — players earn points from each activity

Level Progression — Beginner → Intermediate → Advanced

Skill Feedback Reports — personalised insights on strengths and weaknesses

Scenario Unlock System — new scenarios unlocked based on performance
rewardTiers.access:
Participation Points — awarded for completing scenarios

Reflection Bonus — extra points for explaining decisions

Retry Tokens — allows players to replay scenarios without penalty

Hint Tokens — unlock guidance for difficult scenarios

kirkpatrick.l1:
Students report engagement and satisfaction  
KPI: ≥75% positive feedback in post-session survey
kirkpatrick.l2:
Students improve methodology selection accuracy  
KPI: ≥25% increase between pre-test and post-test
kirkpatrick.l3:
Students correctly apply methodology in later coursework  
KPI: ≥70% correct methodology selection in assignments 4 weeks later
kirkpatrick.l4:
Overall academic performance improves  
KPI: Average course grade improvement ≥ 0.5 letter grade

futureEnhancements: Introduce adaptive scenario complexity based on player performance
Add AI-assisted feedback for deeper explanation of decisions
Expand scenario library with real-world case studies
Integrate with LMS for automated grading and tracking
newVal.dataCollection:
Quantitative Analysis:
Likert-scale and test scores analysed using descriptive statistics (mean, variance, improvement rate).
Qualitative Analysis:
Thematic coding applied to interviews, focus groups, and open-ended responses.
Triangulation:
Multiple data sources combined (analytics + surveys + interviews) to ensure validity and reliability.
Feedback Loop:
Monthly insights shared with stakeholders → iterative improvements applied.
newVal.continuousImprovement: The system will be continuously improved based on collected data, player performance, and feedback to ensure long-term learning effectiveness.
newVal.analyticsplatform: Game execution logs + LMS (e.g., Moodle) integration
newVal.deliverableJust: The selected deliverable areas focus on simulating real-world project management environments where students must choose and apply appropriate methodologies. Agile/Scrum Sprint Simulator enables iterative decision-making under dynamic conditions, while SDLC Phase Manager supports structured planning scenarios aligned with Waterfall methodology. These areas directly address the learning objective of selecting suitable methodologies based on project characteristics and constraints.
newVal.platform: Browser-based web application built using Python (Streamlit v1.x) or a lightweight web framework. The system will run locally or be hosted on a cloud platform (e.g., Render / Heroku alternative) for easy access. No installation is required; players access the simulation via a web browser during class sessions. A facilitator dashboard may be displayed via projector to guide gameplay and discussion.
newVal.narrativeEvolution:
Week 1–2: Introduction and familiarisation with Agile and Waterfall concepts.
Week 3–6: Increasing complexity with dynamic project scenarios and decision-making challenges.
Week 7–10: Competitive phase where players optimise strategies across multiple scenarios.
Week 11–12: Final evaluation and reflection, analysing decisions and outcomes.
newVal.socialRationale:
Social interaction is intentionally designed to support multiple motivational frameworks.

From a Self-Determination Theory (SDT) perspective, social features enhance relatedness by enabling collaboration, discussion, and peer learning. Study clubs and mentoring systems allow students to share strategies and support each other, reflecting real-world project environments.

From a HEXAD perspective, Socialisers are engaged through forums and group discussions, Achievers through leaderboard comparison, and Philanthropists through mentoring and helping others.

From an Octalysis perspective, Core Drive 5 (Social Influence & Relatedness) is activated through peer comparison, feedback, and shared experiences. Core Drive 2 (Accomplishment) is reinforced through leaderboard positioning and visible performance metrics.

These features are not included for engagement alone, but to create structured social learning that improves reflection, decision-making, and knowledge transfer.
newVal.communityStandards:
All interactions must remain respectful, constructive, and academically focused.

Criticism must target decisions and strategies, not individuals. Personal attacks, harassment, or exclusionary behaviour are strictly prohibited.

All social features are moderated automatically (keyword filtering) and by instructors to ensure a safe learning environment.

Students are encouraged to provide evidence-based feedback, justify their decisions, and engage in reflective discussion.

Repeated violations result in temporary restriction from social features.

This policy ensures that social interaction enhances learning rather than creating pressure or negative competition.
newVal.pricingModel: Social and reward systems are explicitly designed to support motivational theories (SDT, HEXAD, Octalysis), ensuring that engagement is driven by meaningful interaction, not superficial gamification elements.
newVal.adaptiveDifficulty:
The system adapts difficulty based on player performance metrics:

- If a player repeatedly selects incorrect methodologies → 
  the system provides hints and simplified scenarios (e.g., clearer project conditions).

- If a player performs consistently well →
  more complex scenarios are introduced (mixed conditions, time pressure, ambiguous requirements).

- Time limits are dynamically adjusted:
  more time for struggling players, less time for advanced players.

This adaptation is rule-based and transparent to ensure fairness and feasibility.
newVal.personalisation:
Personalised Feedback System:
After each scenario, players receive feedback explaining why their decision was correct or incorrect.

Weakness Detection:
The system identifies patterns (e.g., always choosing Agile incorrectly) and highlights learning gaps.

Scenario Recommendation:
Players are guided towards scenarios that target their weak areas.

Optional Customisation:
- Avatar selection (cosmetic only)
- UI preferences (dark/light mode)
- Notification settings

No manipulation or behavioural exploitation is used.

changeLog.newVal: At least 70% of participants correctly select the appropriate project management methodology based on scenario conditions.
Average methodology selection accuracy increases by at least 25% between pre-test and post-test assessments.
At least 80% of participants successfully complete all project simulation scenarios within the allocated session time.
Decision-making performance related to project constraints (time, budget, quality, and risk) improves by at least 20% across multiple rounds.
At least 75% of participants correctly identify requirement volatility and stakeholder complexity during scenario analysis.
Average post-session project management assessment scores improve by at least 30% compared to baseline results.
At least 70% of participants provide evidence-based justifications for their methodology choices during reflection activities.
More than 80% of participants actively participate in collaborative discussion and peer-feedback activities during gameplay sessions.
At least 85% of participants report improved confidence in applying Agile and Waterfall methodologies to real-world project cases.
Course performance related to project management case-study questions improves by at least one letter-grade equivalent after implementation of the gamification system.
